title: Native Integration for Mailchimp Tags

posts.author: vsgarbe (Vinícius Sgarbe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

First of all, I want to say how much I love Monday and the powerful integrations it offers. It’s a tool I recommend to many of my clients, and it has been a game-changer in managing workflows.

That said, I wanted to share an idea that could bring even more value to the integration between Monday and Mailchimp. As you might know, Mailchimp encourages users to maintain a single list of contacts, using tags to segment audiences based on their interests. However, as it stands, the current integration between Monday and Mailchimp doesn’t allow us to add contacts to Mailchimp while assigning tags natively.

This limitation becomes challenging in cases like mine: I use WorkForms to collect information from various audiences with different interests. Without native support for tags, it’s hard to send targeted email campaigns based on these interests, as Mailchimp relies on tags for segmentation. Right now, I have to use tools like Zapier to bridge the gap, which adds complexity and cost.

I believe having a native option to apply tags directly from Monday to Mailchimp would streamline the process, make the integration much more user-friendly, and align perfectly with Mailchimp’s best practices. This enhancement could benefit many users managing diverse campaigns, and it would strengthen the synergy between these two amazing platforms.

I’d love to hear if anyone else has run into a similar need or if the Monday team has plans for this in the future. I’m more than happy to share ideas or insights to help move this forward.

Thanks so much for considering this, and I look forward to hearing everyone’s thoughts!

Best,

Vinícius

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
One workaround (till the Monday team implements this natively) would be to use something like Zapier or Make to sync contacts from Monday to Mailchimp while adding tags for better segmentation.
Cheers,
Harsh

We develop custom solutions on Monday to help elevate your business. Reach out on farioconsulting.com

posts.author: vsgarbe (Vinícius Sgarbe)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you very much! I’m really thinking about using Zapier. Big hug.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @vsgarbe

We developed an app called Mailchimp Integration Plus that provides a robust integration with Mailchimp. Amongst other capabilities, it allows to do full mapping of fields from monday to Mailchimp, including custom fields for merge tags. Here’s an example →

Screenshot 2024-05-13 at 12.17.122714×1440 269 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/10000454

Hope that helps!

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @vsgarbe,

Some great options already posted in this thread. But if you’re still looking for another solution on this, you can now integrate Mailchimp into monday using the Data Importer monday app.

A few things you can do with Mailchimp using Data Importer:

  • Bulk import your entire contact list from an audience.
  • Import various contact fields (e.g. name, email, status, custom merge fields and more) plus stats (e.g. average open rate)
  • Schedule imports to keep your monday board in sync when new contacts are added to Mailchimp.

I’ve also created a step-by-step tutorial for more details on how to import Mailchimp contacts

Monday Mailchimp Integration1992×1084 141 KB

posts.author: vsgarbe (Vinícius Sgarbe)
posts.cookedHtml: Dear friend, thank you so much for the recommendation. It truly means a lot!
